A Saree House

Six yards, one continuous thread, no cuts. Every saree we choose to carry is, in its own way, a beginning — of an evening, a ceremony, a story worn forward.

Scroll — the drape continues
Our Beginning

Not a trend.
A starting point.

Aarambh means beginning — and that's exactly what a saree is built to be. No seams, no shortcuts, just one uncut length of cloth waiting to be draped a hundred different ways.

We built this house on that idea. Some pieces come from handloom clusters that have woven the same patterns for generations. Others are cut with a more contemporary eye. All of them are chosen because they're worth beginning something in.

Three Ways In

Find your starting thread

Handloom Heritage

Kanjivarams, Bandhanis, Jamdanis — woven the slow way, by hand, the way they've always been made.

Heirloom weaves

The Contemporary Edit

Pre-draped silhouettes, unexpected colour pairings, fabrics cut for the way sarees are actually worn today.

Modern drapes

Everyday Aarambh

Cottons and easy synthetics for ordinary mornings — because every day deserves its own small beginning too.

Daily wear
